title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config, verify test coverage/evidence for the Phase 0 handoff pipeline change. Compare against the 7-item test plan and acceptance criteria in ~/.claude/cache/handoff-phase0-codex.md and ~/.claude/cache/dev-spec-phase0-handoff-pipeline.md. Return missing test...
summary_text: **Findings** - Current Kernel local authority is still single-lane: `codex-kernel-guard launch` writes receipt/ledger then `exec`s Codex thread only, so multi-agent execution is not happening in the launch path ([codex-kernel-guard.sh:492](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:492), [codex-kernel-guard.sh:545](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:545), [kernel-codex-thread.sh:110](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-codex-thread.sh:110)). This is a workflow violation for required multi-agent orchestration. - Real dispatch substrate exists (`kernel-scheduler` + `kernel-runtime-run-driver`) but is not wired into `codex-kernel-guard launch` ([kernel-scheduler.sh:262](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh:262), [kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh:160](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh:160)). - Evidence is not auditable enough: phase gates mostly trust provider success counters, and tests commonly inject `record-provider` directly (synthetic evidence) ([kernel-phase-gate.sh:107](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-phase-gate.sh:107), [test-kernel-phase-gate.sh:51](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-phase-gate.sh:51)). - Grok/xAI has matrix/harness support but no Kernel local launch execution path ([build-agent-matrix.sh:367](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/build-agent-matrix.sh:367), [ci-agent-runner.sh:395](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/ci-agent-runner.sh:395), [codex-kernel-guard.sh:104](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:104)). **Minimal code changes** 1. Wire `codex-kernel-guard launch` to dispatch one approved topology through scheduler/run-driver before/alongside Codex thread launch (small change in [codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh) near `cmd_launch`). 2. Add one Kernel-owned provider bundle executor (`scripts/lib/kernel-multi-provider-exec.sh`) that actually runs `glm` + specialist lanes via existing wrappers and emits artifacts; call it from scheduler topology `command_string`. 3. Add explicit xAI lane executor for Kernel runtime (`scripts/lib/kernel-xai-exec.sh`) and include `xai` in provider normalization/selection where launch receipts are built. 4. Extend ledger provider records to include provenance (`source=runtime-exec|manual`, `trace_id`, `command_hash`), then make phase/health gates require `runtime-exec` evidence for pass ([kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh), [kernel-phase-gate.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-phase-gate.sh), [kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh)). 5. Replace synthetic evidence tests with simulation-executable evidence tests: add one end-to-end scheduler/run-driver test using stub CLIs/APIs to prove auditable provider execution across required lanes, and stop using direct `record-provider` in gate-critical tests.
